babyDevelopment: The baby is now fully grown but continues to gain weight—about an ounce a day. Most babies graduate from practicing breathing to having mature lungs. The skull bones remain soft and decoupled for a safer passage through the birth canal.
bodyChanges: You might feel 'lightening' more clearly as the baby drops into the pelvic cavity. Pelvic pressure will increase, making walking more a 'waddle.' You likely have weekly appointments now. Braxton Hicks may become more intense.
tips: Finalize your hospital bag and ensure your car seat is installed
Monitor baby's movement several times a day
Prepare some freezer meals for after the baby arrives

do: Stay active with short, gentle walks
Focus on good posture to manage pelvic pressure
dont: Travel far from your delivery facility
Ignore signs of severe, persistent swelling

travelAdvisory.note: Avoid all non-essential travel; stay close to home.

milestoneDescription: Baby is reaching full term status as lungs and most systems are fully mature.
